Tests the immutability of the Number prototype by asserting that it cannot be deleted.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the immutability of the `Number.prototype` object in JavaScript. The `delete` operator is used to attempt the removal of `Number.prototype`, and the assertion checks that this operation returns `false`, confirming that `Number.prototype` cannot be deleted. This behavior is consistent with the ECMAScript specification, which defines `Number.prototype` as a non-configurable property. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on verifying the non-deletable nature of `Number.prototype`.
